summary: CPU functional tests failed because the PR intentionally introduced test failures in matmul.cpp to observe the new run_parallel.py behavior that prints the first 10 failed tests in CI output.

root_cause.explanation: The PR '[TESTS][GHA] run_parallel.py print first 10 failed tests' deliberately injects GTEST_FAIL() calls in src/plugins/intel_cpu/tests/functional/custom/single_layer_tests/classes/matmul.cpp at line 196 with message 'intentional failure to observe new run_parallel.py output'. This causes 645 MatMulLayerCPUTest tests to fail. The purpose is to validate that run_parallel.py now correctly prints the first 10 failed test logs directly in the GHA workflow output (ERROR:test_parallel_runner:Printing [10/645] failed tests), eliminating the need to download artifacts to see failures.
evidence: matmul.cpp(196): error: Failed - intentional failure to observe new run_parallel.py output

reproduction: Apply the PR #36469 changes, build ov_cpu_func_tests.exe, and run it with --gtest_filter=*smoke*MatMul*. The intentional GTEST_FAIL() in matmul.cpp will trigger the failures.
recommended_actions: Review the new run_parallel.py output format in the job log to confirm it meets expectations.
Remove the intentional test failures from matmul.cpp before merging (the commit message 'Fail tests intentionally to observe new behavior' confirms this is temporary).
Verify the run_parallel.py change correctly limits output to the first 10 failed tests.
prevention: When testing CI script behavior changes, use a dedicated test flag or mock environment rather than injecting intentional failures in production test code.
